behaviors.description: `=SUM(A1:A10)` returns the correct total for values in `A1..A10`.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is in module handoff (parser -> range resolver -> function evaluator -> cell store), not just SUM math. Behavior text does not require OS/network/subprocess or AI output, so higher than Integration is disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Create a workbook with numeric values in `A1:A10`, set another cell to `=SUM(A1:A10)`, run engine evaluation, and assert the computed value equals the arithmetic total.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing another formula cell (e.g., `=B1+1`, `B1:=A1*2`) computes the correct cascaded value.
behaviors.justification: This specifically tests cross-cell dependency resolution and cascaded evaluation across components. No explicit real-infra or model dependency in the behavior text, so System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Set `A1=3`, `B1==A1*2`, `C1==B1+1`; evaluate workbook and assert `B1=6` and `C1=7`.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency (`A1 -> B1 -> A1`) is detected and returns a circular-reference error.
behaviors.justification: The failure appears when dependency graph construction and evaluator interaction are exercised together. Behavior text does not require external contract checks or real infrastructure, so not System.
behaviors.test_description: Define `A1==B1+1` and `B1==A1+1`, run evaluation, and assert a circular-reference error is produced (and evaluation terminates).

behaviors.description: `VLOOKUP` exact match returns correct value; missing key returns error.
behaviors.justification: This behavior includes both happy/error paths through formula parsing, table lookup wiring, and engine error propagation. No OS/network/subprocess/AI requirement is stated, so higher levels are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a lookup table range, evaluate `VLOOKUP` with an existing key and then a missing key, and assert correct value in first case and expected lookup error in second.

behaviors.description: Nested `IF` conditions evaluate to correct branch (`high`/`medium`/`low`).
behaviors.justification: This is primarily expression semantics within evaluator logic (branching and nesting). Behavior text does not require cross-module state handoff beyond one evaluator boundary, nor real infra/AI.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test nested IF expression evaluation for representative `A1` values (e.g., 5, 15, 25) and assert outputs `low`, `medium`, `high`.

behaviors.description: `CONCATENATE` joins values with separator and skips empty cells.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is function-level string handling/empty-cell semantics and can be validated at a single evaluator/function boundary. No explicit module orchestration or real infra requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test `CONCATENATE` with mixed empty/non-empty inputs and separator; assert output omits empty entries rather than inserting blank tokens.

behaviors.description: Cross-sheet reference (`=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves correctly when sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: This failure mode is about inter-module handoff (sheet registry/name resolution + reference evaluator). No real OS/network/subprocess or AI dependency in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Create `Sheet1` and `Sheet2`, set `Sheet2!A1=10`, set `Sheet1!A1==Sheet2!A1+1`, evaluate, and assert `Sheet1!A1=11`.

behaviors.description: When a referenced value changes, dependents recalculate in correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly about dependency graph invalidation and recomputation sequencing across modules. Behavior text does not require external-system contract or AI output.
behaviors.test_description: Build a dependency chain/DAG (including branching), evaluate once, mutate an upstream cell, trigger recalculation, and assert all downstream values match expected results consistent with topological recompute.

behaviors.description: Undo after formula edit restores both formula text and previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on interaction between edit history, formula storage, dependency tracking, and recalculation state. No explicit real infra or AI dependency, so System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Set a formula and dependent cells, capture baseline values, edit formula and re-evaluate, perform undo, then assert formula text and all affected dependent values return to baseline.

behaviors.description: Save workbook to disk and load back with identical formulas/values, including floating-point precision.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires proving an externally visible side effect via round-trip persistence/readback across a real external boundary (disk file). This triggers the System triad round-trip rule; unit/integration-only with fakes can miss true persistence/serialization issues.
behaviors.test_description: Write a workbook with representative formulas and floating-point values to an actual file path, reload from disk using normal load path, and assert exact formula text and expected numeric equivalence/precision constraints after round-trip.
